fired_locations: POC.c:59 (in affs_remount_buggy, kstrdup -> new_opts leaked on success-path returns)
notes: Acted-as-LLM (Claude Opus 4.7 in-session) for POC + query generation.
POC reuses the lu-5-rep1 structural skeleton (two-function _buggy/_fixed; bug = kstrdup(new_opts) freed only on parse_options error path, leaked on success-path returns).
Seed-slice was extended with a postfix-twin function so per-file F1.release/F5.return/F2.if counts equal the 2-function POC; first POC passed verifier-v1 strictly without regen.

Query is generic: detects any allocator (kstrdup/kmalloc/kzalloc/...) whose receiver is freed in some basic block but is not dominated by the free on at least one return path (CFG-based dominance via bbDominates).
Single compile+analyze round; query passed POC oracle on first attempt (no repair iter needed).
rep4 variance: BasicBlock-dominance phrasing of the leak predicate (vs textual ordering / call-graph traversal in other reps).
